course_description: Introductory programming course for first-year students.
course_learning_outcomes: Write basic programs and debug simple applications.
learning_activities_and_teaching_methods: Lectures plus weekly coding labs and short practice exercises.
assessment: Homework assignments 40%; lab participation 20%; final project 40%.
course_contents: Variables, control flow, functions, basic data structures, and testing.
background_and_overall_aim: Build a solid foundation in computational thinking and practical coding.
contact_details: Instructor: Elena Novak; email: elena.novak@dummy.edu; Office hours: Tue 10:00-12:00.

course_description: Mathematical foundations for computing and software design.
course_learning_outcomes: Reason about logic proofs sets graphs and combinatorics.
learning_activities_and_teaching_methods: Lectures with problem-solving seminars and guided worksheet sessions.
course_contents: Logic, sets, relations, counting, proof techniques, and graph basics.
background_and_overall_aim: Support later computer science courses with rigorous mathematical tools.
contact_details: Instructor: Martin Hale; email: martin.hale@dummy.edu; Office hours: Wed 13:00-14:30.

course_description: Statistics foundations for evidence-based data analysis.
course_learning_outcomes: Run descriptive and inferential analysis on small datasets.
learning_activities_and_teaching_methods: Lectures, software tutorials, and supervised data analysis workshops.
course_contents: Probability, sampling, estimation, hypothesis testing, and regression basics.
background_and_overall_aim: Introduce core statistical thinking for future data science work.
contact_details: Instructor: Sofia Marin; email: sofia.marin@dummy.edu; Office hours: Mon 15:00-16:30.

course_description: Intermediate programming course focused on efficiency and abstraction.
course_learning_outcomes: Implement efficient data structures and analyze their complexity.
learning_activities_and_teaching_methods: Lectures, code reviews, lab sessions, and weekly implementation tasks.
course_contents: Lists, stacks, queues, trees, hash tables, recursion, and complexity.
background_and_overall_aim: Prepare students for advanced software engineering and systems courses.
contact_details: Instructor: Elena Novak; email: elena.novak@dummy.edu; Office hours: Tue 10:00-12:00.

course_description: Applied introduction to supervised and unsupervised machine learning.
course_learning_outcomes: Train and evaluate predictive models responsibly.
learning_activities_and_teaching_methods: Lectures combined with notebook-based labs and group discussions.
course_contents: Regression, classification, clustering, model evaluation, and feature engineering.
background_and_overall_aim: Develop practical model-building skills and critical evaluation habits.
contact_details: Instructor: Sofia Marin; email: sofia.marin@dummy.edu; Office hours: Mon 15:00-16:30.

course_description: Reporting and dashboard design for business decision-making.
course_learning_outcomes: Create dashboards and communicate KPI-driven insights.
learning_activities_and_teaching_methods: Case-based lectures, dashboard workshops, and presentation coaching.
assessment: Dashboard portfolio 50%; case presentation 20%; final report 30%.
course_contents: Data visualization, KPI design, reporting workflows, and stakeholder communication.
background_and_overall_aim: Teach students to turn data into operational business insights.
contact_details: Instructor: Laura Kent; email: laura.kent@dummy.edu; Office hours: Thu 11:00-12:30.

course_description: Hands-on course on deploying reliable AI-enabled services.
course_learning_outcomes: Build and deploy AI pipelines with monitoring and evaluation.
learning_activities_and_teaching_methods: Seminars, system design workshops, deployment labs, and research discussions.
assessment: Implementation project 40%; technical report 30%; oral presentation 30%.
course_contents: Model serving, pipeline orchestration, monitoring, evaluation, and deployment patterns.
background_and_overall_aim: Bridge advanced machine learning with production-oriented AI system design.
contact_details: Instructor: Pavel Ilic; email: pavel.ilic@dummy.edu; Office hours: Fri 09:30-11:00.

course_description: Relational database design and practical SQL for real systems.
course_learning_outcomes: Design normalized schemas and write efficient SQL queries.
learning_activities_and_teaching_methods: Lectures, SQL labs, schema design studios, and short reading responses.
assessment: SQL exercises 35%; schema project 25%; final exam 40%.
course_contents: ER modeling, normalization, transactions, indexes, and query design.
background_and_overall_aim: Provide a solid base for data engineering and application back-end work.
contact_details: Instructor: Martin Hale; email: martin.hale@dummy.edu; Office hours: Wed 13:00-14:30.
